jokes.text: I stake my reputation on this. Selina Meyer will never be vice president ever, ever again.
jokes.setup: Mike emphatically stakes his reputation that Selina will never be VP again
jokes.punchline: The irony that she will indeed become VP again, making this a career-ending statement
jokes.explanation: Classic dramatic irony setup that pays off brilliantly when Selina does become VP. Mike's overconfident declaration becomes his epitaph.

jokes.text: Pot, kettle.
jokes.setup: Selina calls Tom an accidental president
jokes.punchline: Tom's terse acknowledgment that she was also an accidental president
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character-appropriate comeback. Two words that deflate Selina's criticism completely.

jokes.text: That's literally the least you can do.
jokes.setup: Tom offers her Secretary of State as 'the least he can do'
jokes.punchline: Selina's literal interpretation that VP is actually the lesser position
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that shows Selina's desperation and Tom's manipulation. The literal interpretation creates the humor.

jokes.text: I wouldn't be your veep if there were a grassy knoll full of Jodie Foster fans in the front row at your inauguration.
jokes.setup: Selina rejecting the VP position emphatically
jokes.punchline: Combining JFK assassination reference with Reagan assassination attempt in one elaborate threat scenario
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and complex reference that combines two assassination attempts into one impossibly specific scenario. Peak Veep writing.

jokes.text: General George Washington could climb out of his grave right now and I would rather eat out his zombified wooden asshole twice a day than be his vice fucking... fucking anything!
jokes.setup: Continuing her emphatic rejection of any VP role
jokes.punchline: Escalating to an impossibly grotesque scenario involving zombie Washington's wooden teeth
jokes.explanation: This is legendary Veep dialogue - combining historical reference, scatological humor, and impossible escalation. The 'wooden asshole' detail is genius.

jokes.text: Out of how many? - 44. You were right ahead of James Buchanan, who many feel caused the Civil War.
jokes.setup: Kent announces she's rated 43rd most effective president
jokes.punchline: The devastating reveal that there are only 44 presidents, making her second-worst in history
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline structure that devastates Selina. The Buchanan reference adds historical weight to the insult.

jokes.text: Kent, can you give a girl some warning next time before you jam it in the back door?
jokes.setup: Kent delivering bad news without preamble
jokes.punchline: Selina's inadvertently sexual metaphor for receiving bad news
jokes.explanation: Typical Veep sexual innuendo, well-delivered but not particularly original. Gets the job done.

jokes.text: Oh, God. I can't take that much acoustic guitar.
jokes.setup: Discussion of first White House lesbian wedding
jokes.punchline: Selina's shallow concern about stereotypical lesbian wedding music
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor that reveals Selina's priorities. The stereotype is specific enough to feel earned.

jokes.text: Too bad Goebbels killed himself.
jokes.setup: Dan needs an agent for his brand
jokes.punchline: Casually suggesting Nazi propaganda minister as ideal representation
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Dan's moral bankruptcy in one casual Nazi reference. The economy is brutal and character-perfect.

jokes.text: The only president to pee sitting down since FDR.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Selina's legacy
jokes.punchline: Comparing her emasculation to FDR's physical disability
jokes.explanation: Dark humor connecting gender, power, and disability. The FDR reference adds historical weight to the insult.

jokes.text: Well, I pee sitting down sometimes if it's gonna be a longy.
jokes.setup: Ben's emasculating comment about Selina
jokes.punchline: Ben's matter-of-fact admission of his own bathroom habits
jokes.explanation: Ben's casual oversharing deflates his own insult. The matter-of-fact delivery sells it.

jokes.text: Wherever they have prostitutes. These guys could help you.
jokes.setup: Looking for Qataris to help with Tibet
jokes.punchline: Selina's casual assumption about where to find Middle Eastern diplomats
jokes.explanation: Reveals Selina's casual prejudices and desperation. The stereotype feels a bit cheap but fits her character.

jokes.text: No, New Hampshire is just a fancy word for 'it's cold outside, so I don't shave my pubes.'
jokes.setup: Discussion of New Hampshire interns
jokes.punchline: Jonah's reductive geographical assessment based on grooming habits
jokes.explanation: Classic Jonah crude humor. The geographic stereotype is specific but relies on cheap shock value.

jokes.text: It's really more of a copresident position. Wouldn't you agree with that, Ben? - Sure.
jokes.setup: Selina desperately trying to spin her VP return as powerful
jokes.punchline: Ben's reluctant, unconvincing agreement to obvious spin
jokes.explanation: The cringe comes from everyone knowing this is pathetic spin. Ben's reluctant 'Sure' is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Oh, right, no touchy the ladies. Oh, no, no. It's fine. It's private. Just don't let the lunatics back home know.
jokes.setup: Cultural sensitivity moment with Chinese official
jokes.punchline: His casual dismissal of his own people as 'lunatics' while maintaining diplomatic facade
jokes.explanation: Shows diplomatic hypocrisy and cultural tensions. The 'lunatics back home' line has nice bite.

jokes.text: You have decided to throw away every last shred of dignity you have and accept my offer of the vice presidency. - I... I guess so, yeah.
jokes.setup: Tom accurately describing Selina's capitulation
jokes.punchline: Selina's weak, defeated agreement to the harsh but accurate assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures the power dynamic and Selina's humiliation. Tom's cruelty and her acceptance are both character-perfect.

jokes.text: It's like a shitty Groundhog Day.
jokes.setup: Selina becoming VP again after swearing she wouldn't
jokes.punchline: Perfect pop culture reference for being trapped in a repeating nightmare
jokes.explanation: Perfect metaphor for her situation. The 'shitty' qualifier makes it specifically Selina's version of the movie.

jokes.text: Sir, by any objective standards, Colt, Brady, and Mason are unequivocally hot, not not.
jokes.setup: Jonah complaining about the male interns
jokes.punchline: Richard's earnest defense using his characteristic verbal tics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Richard dialogue with the 'not not' construction. His earnest confusion makes the misunderstanding funnier.

jokes.text: Hot interns means girls. How the fuck do you not know that?
jokes.setup: Richard hired attractive male interns instead of female ones
jokes.punchline: Jonah's incredulous reaction to Richard's literal interpretation
jokes.explanation: Classic Jonah being terrible, but the sexism is so blatant it's almost too easy. Richard's innocence sells it.

jokes.text: I want you guys to get used to two things... killing it daily on the Hill and nights rated PG-squirteen.
jokes.setup: Jonah welcoming his new male interns
jokes.punchline: His made-up rating system combining PG-13 with 'squirt' for sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Typical Jonah crude wordplay. The 'PG-squirteen' construction is creative but relies on juvenile humor.

jokes.text: But feel free to follow along on the television or perhaps on one of the many bugs that you've planted throughout the White House
jokes.setup: Excusing herself from diplomatic meeting
jokes.punchline: Casual accusation of Chinese espionage
jokes.explanation: Selina's diplomatic bomb-dropping. Inappropriate but character-consistent boundary crossing.

jokes.text: Come on, you page-diddling Hawaiian fat fuck.
jokes.setup: Watching Senate vote, needing specific senator's support
jokes.punchline: Ben's characteristic multi-layered insult combining scandal reference, geography, and body shaming
jokes.explanation: Classic Ben insult with multiple offensive layers. The 'page-diddling' reference is specific to DC scandal culture.

jokes.text: Thank you, Summerlin, you senile old piss sponge.
jokes.setup: Getting a favorable vote from elderly senator
jokes.punchline: Tom's backhanded gratitude with inventive geriatric insult
jokes.explanation: Creative insult that's both grateful and insulting. 'Piss sponge' is memorably disgusting.

jokes.text: Now you know what it feels like.
jokes.setup: Tom loses the presidency in a stunning reversal
jokes.punchline: Selina's simple, devastating acknowledgment of cosmic justice
jokes.explanation: Perfect moment of karmic justice. The economy is brutal - four words that encapsulate everything.

jokes.text: Gary's entire meltdown: 'That magnificent woman counted on you and you losers let her down... All you fucking cared about was your stupid, bad selves... I did my job! I fucking cared!'
jokes.setup: Gary finally snapping after years of watching Selina be mistreated
jokes.punchline: His raw emotional breakdown contrasted with his usual servile behavior
jokes.explanation: Emotionally devastating and hilarious. Gary's devotion finally exploding is both pathetic and heroic. Peak character work.

jokes.text: Your numbers are garbage. Your speeches, garbage.
jokes.setup: Gary continuing his emotional breakdown
jokes.punchline: Gary calling everyone's work garbage while defending Selina
jokes.explanation: Gary's repetitive 'garbage' assessment building to climax of his breakdown. Good escalation.

jokes.text: Well, that just kind of made this whole year worth it.
jokes.setup: Gary's explosive emotional breakdown
jokes.punchline: Ben's typically understated appreciation for the drama
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben response - finding entertainment in others' misery. The understatement makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Yeah, she seems really, really large for a baby. - I was a tall kid when I was three.
jokes.setup: Looking at Mike's 'baby' Ellen
jokes.punchline: Mike's oblivious response revealing Ellen is actually three, not a baby
jokes.explanation: Classic Mike obliviousness. The slow reveal that this isn't actually a baby is well-structured.

jokes.text: So what's the reason now, then?
jokes.setup: Mike explaining he was unusually tall as a child
jokes.punchline: Selina's implication that something is still wrong with him now
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina cruelty with great economy. The implication is devastating and perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Mine takes place in an alternate timeline where overweight, alcoholic ex-chiefs of staff are considered a sexual prize.
jokes.setup: Dan pitching a young adult fiction idea
jokes.punchline: Ben's self-deprecating alternate universe where he'd be desirable
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-aware humor from Ben. The specificity of 'overweight, alcoholic ex-chiefs of staff' is perfect.

jokes.text: Mine takes place in an alternate timeline where overweight, alcoholic ex-chiefs of staff are considered a sexual prize
jokes.setup: Discussion of young adult fiction ideas
jokes.punchline: Ben's brutally self-aware alternate reality
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-deprecating humor from Ben with specific details that make it funnier. Great character awareness.

jokes.text: Alternate timelines are a crutch for lazy sci-fi premises, e.g., 'Star Trek' with the exception of 'Deep Space Nine.'
jokes.setup: Ben's alternate timeline pitch
jokes.punchline: Kent's pedantic sci-fi criticism with specific Trek knowledge
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kent pedantry with surprising nerd knowledge. The DS9 exception is a nice touch for genre fans.

jokes.text: MSNBC has just learned that President-Elect Montez plans to nominate Wall Street banker Charlie Baird... President Meyer dated the banker briefly late last year.
jokes.setup: News about Montez's cabinet picks
jokes.punchline: The revelation that Selina's ex-boyfriend is getting a major position
jokes.explanation: Another twist of the knife for Selina. The casual mention of their dating adds personal humiliation.

jokes.text: I don't even remember how to drive.
jokes.setup: Selina facing life after the presidency
jokes.punchline: Her realization of how disconnected she's become from normal life
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures the isolation of high office. The simple confession is both funny and sad.

jokes.text: I don't even remember how to drive. I need a wallet. And stamps. I've got to get stamps
jokes.setup: Selina facing civilian life
jokes.punchline: Basic life skills she's forgotten or never had
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing how divorced from normal life politicians become. Stamps detail is perfect.

jokes.text: And then it turned out to be the 12 loneliest months of my life.
jokes.setup: Selina reflecting on achieving her childhood dream
jokes.punchline: The devastating reality that getting everything she wanted made her miserable
jokes.explanation: Devastating and true. The irony of achieving your dream only to find emptiness is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: Maybe my auntie was actually my real mother and my mother was actually my grandmother. Ah, wow, that's starting to make a whole lot of sense. Everybody knew about it but me, too.
jokes.setup: Richard relating to Selina's loneliness through family stories
jokes.punchline: His slow realization of his own family's deception in real time
jokes.explanation: Perfect Richard moment - his innocent confusion leading to dark family revelation. The escalating realization is well-structured.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, I hope I didn't fuck Richard.
jokes.setup: Selina waking up disoriented after emotional breakdown
jokes.punchline: Her horrified concern about what happened with innocent Richard
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina self-absorption - her first concern is her own reputation. The Richard pairing is absurd.

jokes.text: Do you realize the whole goddamn Rayburn House Office Building can hear you and your twink army in here cock scraping each other's esophagi?
jokes.setup: Male interns being too loud playing sports
jokes.punchline: Will's over-the-top sexual interpretation of innocent roughhousing
jokes.explanation: Creatively disgusting imagery with 'cock scraping esophagi.' The medical terminology makes it worse/better.

jokes.text: You look like Clark Kent if they dug up Christopher Reeve's corpse to play the part.
jokes.setup: Criticizing Colt's glasses
jokes.punchline: Combining Superman reference with Christopher Reeve's tragic death
jokes.explanation: Brutally dark humor combining pop culture with tragedy. The specificity makes it memorable and horrible.

jokes.text: And as for you ladies, this is DC, not Miami.
jokes.setup: Will criticizing the male interns' casual dress
jokes.punchline: Calling them 'ladies' while making geographical fashion distinction
jokes.explanation: Standard emasculating insult with geographic snobbery. Gets the job done but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: My fucking nuts!
jokes.setup: Basketball accident during the male intern bonding
jokes.punchline: Colt's pained exclamation after getting hit in the groin
jokes.explanation: Basic slapstick humor. Gets a physical laugh but requires no craft or creativity.

jokes.text: Mike, she has the head circumference of a six-year-old. - I'm six.
jokes.setup: Kent making analytical observation about baby Ellen's size
jokes.punchline: Ellen's matter-of-fact correction revealing she's not a baby at all
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the running gag. Ellen's deadpan delivery and Kent's inadvertent accuracy create great timing.

jokes.text: Girls will cost you later. Mm, girls are the worst
jokes.setup: Discussion of boys vs girls
jokes.punchline: Two powerful women agreeing girls are the worst
jokes.explanation: Ironic moment of two successful women bonding over misogyny. Self-aware but not exceptional.

jokes.text: And where exactly in Ohio are you from, Laura?
jokes.setup: Montez talking about being from Mexico and New Mexico
jokes.punchline: Selina's deliberate confusion/slight about Montez's background
jokes.explanation: Classic Selina passive-aggressive racism disguised as confusion. The geographic mix-up is deliberately insulting.

jokes.text: Your head is so far up Montez's ass. Next time it's Alejandro's birthday, he's gonna come all over your face.
jokes.setup: Selina confronting Andrew about betraying her
jokes.punchline: Combining political betrayal with crude sexual imagery involving Montez's family
jokes.explanation: Crude but creative insult combining politics and sex. The Alejandro reference is specific but the imagery relies on shock value.

jokes.text: You're not gonna believe this, but she just freed the lamas.
jokes.setup: Montez achieving what Selina desperately wanted
jokes.punchline: The casual delivery of Selina's ultimate nightmare - Montez succeeding instantly
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that twists the knife. Montez achieving Selina's legacy dream immediately is brutal irony.

jokes.text: You're not gonna believe this, but she just freed the lamas
jokes.setup: Selina's dream of freeing Tibet for her legacy
jokes.punchline: Montez accomplishes it minutes into her presidency
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony - Montez effortlessly achieves what Selina desperately wanted. Great callback structure.

jokes.text: I had no idea her tits were that big.
jokes.setup: Being asked about Montez's inaugural speech
jokes.punchline: Tom's completely inappropriate focus on her physical attributes
jokes.explanation: Basic sexist humor that reveals character but isn't particularly clever. The timing after 'historic day for womankind' adds irony.

jokes.text: Well, I've always dreamed of living in a small town, having a little place on Main Street, hanging a shingle out front, and running my own little multibillion-dollar hedge fund.
jokes.setup: Asked about his future plans after losing the presidency
jokes.punchline: The contradiction between small-town simplicity and massive financial operation
jokes.explanation: Excellent contrast between folksy imagery and corporate greed. The 'little multibillion-dollar' contradiction is perfect.

jokes.text: Well, I've always dreamed of living in a small town, having a little place on Main Street, hanging a shingle out front, and running my own little multibillion-dollar hedge fund
jokes.setup: Tom describing simple small-town dreams
jokes.punchline: The 'simple' business is a multibillion-dollar hedge fund
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection from folksy simplicity to massive wealth. Great structure and timing.

jokes.text: Or winning presidential elections. - Well, that's not true. - Yeah, it is.
jokes.setup: Selina self-deprecating about not being good at goodbyes
jokes.punchline: The back-and-forth where Gary tries to comfort her but she insists on her failure
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Selina's self-awareness and Gary's loyalty. The correction/re-correction is well-timed.

jokes.text: What am I walking on? - Grass, ma'am.
jokes.setup: Selina forced to exit the broken helicopter
jokes.punchline: Her complete disconnect from basic outdoor experiences
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Selina's privileged disconnect from normal life. The simple exchange is devastating.

jokes.text: but you have a lump on your left testicle. I just want to biopsy it and we can take it from there. - Take what from where?
jokes.setup: Doctor delivering serious medical news to Jonah
jokes.punchline: Jonah's oblivious confusion about the implications
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Jonah's health scare. His obliviousness to medical euphemism is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I'm married. I have the ring hiding under the gloves. No, I saw it. No. Oh
jokes.setup: Jonah asking doctor to inaugural ball
jokes.punchline: Doctor's increasingly firm rejection
jokes.explanation: Standard rejection scene with escalating awkwardness. Character-consistent Jonah obliviousness.

jokes.text: Selina, good news. We found the missing Nevada ballots. You won.
jokes.setup: Final moments as Selina processes leaving office
jokes.punchline: The ultimate cruel irony - she actually won, but it's too late
jokes.explanation: Perfect devastating ending. The timing, the casual delivery, the cosmic cruelty - this is peak Veep writing. Series finale worthy.
